metadata.title:
GRAVES PEOPLE SLOW


TO PAY ON FRANCHISES

pages.raw_text:
The statement also revealed the
fact that a so year franchise grant
ed to the Consumers Light & Fuel
Co. has. no owner. 1t was granted
to the company and no individual
was named. Among the provisions
is one which agrees that each year
after. it is in operation $4,000 must
be paid to- the city and after 1920,
$4,000 a. year.


The comptroller has not been
able to locate an owner Of the
franchise and it is presumed that
the concern never undertook to do
business.


The Interstate Telephone Co.,
which operates farmers lines ra-
dating from this city, is in arrears
since 1905 to an amount Of $50 a
year, which would total 5000. Steps
to force a payment are under con-
sideration.


1t is not generally known, bat
the Interstate Fair association
owns a street railway franchise on
Sprague av. from Napa st. to the
grounds, and by the provisions OF
it should pay the city $50 a year
from 1902. No payment has ever
been made and there is now due
$3.50. The fair association was
given the franchise July %, 1904
This line is now operated by the
Washington Water Power Co.
